instruction: Open ~/Pictures/color.png in Preview, convert it to grayscale, and save it to ~/Pictures/gray.png.
source: cove-original (design 047 §5 image fidelity); a color source vs a grayscale gold always differ, so image_similar cleanly separates a correct convert from a no-op despite the Preview re-encode

args: mkdir -p "$HOME/Pictures"; printf 'iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAgAAAAICAIAAABLbSncAAAAGElEQVR4nGO4IycHR0jMOwxUlEDm0EgCAEKdRgHZX5G5AAAAAElFTkSuQmCC' | base64 --decode > "$HOME/Pictures/color.png"; sips -s format png -m '/System/Library/ColorSync/Profiles/Generic Gray Profile.icc' "$HOME/Pictures/color.png" --out "$HOME/Pictures/gold.png" >/dev/null 2>&1; cp "$HOME/Pictures/color.png" "$HOME/Pictures/gray.png"
